html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Three — Part Two

The Torah — Third Book

VAYIKRA

And He Called

Chapters 17 through 27

The Holiness Code — The Feasts of YHWH — The Jubilee Year

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QLeva (4Q23) · 4QLevb (4Q24) · 4QLevc (4Q25) · 4QLevd (4Q26)

4QLeve (4Q26a) · 4QLevf (4Q26b) · 11QLeva (11Q1)

4QpaleoLev-Num (4Q23) — Paleo-Hebrew script

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE HOLINESS CODE — CHAPTERS 17 THROUGH 22

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 17 Blood and Slaughter — The Prohibition of Blood — The Altar

17:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

17:2 Speak to Aharon and his sons and to all the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: This is the thing that YHWH has commanded.

17:3 If any one of the house of Yisra'EL kills an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp, or kills it outside the camp,

17:4 And does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer it as an offering to YHWH in front of the Mishkan of YHWH, bloodguilt shall be imputed to that man. He has shed blood, and that man shall be cut off from among his people.

17:7 So they shall no more sacrifice their sacrifices to goat demons, after whom they whore. This shall be a statute forever for them throughout their generations.

17:10 If any one of the house of Yisra'EL or of the strangers who sojourn among them eats any blood, I will set my face against that person who eats blood and will cut him off from among his people.

17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it for you on the altar to make atonement for your souls, for it is the blood that makes atonement by the life.

17:12 Therefore I have said to the people of Yisra'EL: No person among you shall eat blood, neither shall any stranger who sojourns among you eat blood.

17:14 For the life of every creature is its blood — its blood is its life. Therefore I have said to the people of Yisra'EL: You shall not eat the blood of any creature, for the life of every creature is its blood. Whoever eats it shall be cut off.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 17.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17:11 — the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it for you on the altar to make atonement for your souls, for it is the blood that makes atonement by the life. This is the foundational statement of the entire sacrificial theology of Vayikra. Blood is life. Life belongs to YHWH. YHWH gives blood for the altar — not man taking it for himself. Atonement operates because YHWH designates blood as the instrument of covenant restoration. The prohibition of consuming blood and the use of blood for atonement are two sides of the same theological principle: blood-as-life belongs exclusively to YHWH.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 18 Forbidden Sexual Relations — The Holiness of the Land

18:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

18:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: I am YHWH your Creator.

18:3 You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Kena'an, to which I am bringing you. You shall not walk in their statutes.

18:4 You shall follow my rules and keep my statutes and walk in them. I am YHWH your Creator.

18:5 You shall therefore keep my statutes and my rules — if a person does them, he shall live by them. I am YHWH.

18:6 None of you shall approach any one of his close relatives to uncover nakedness. I am YHWH.

18:19 You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.

18:20 And you shall not lie sexually with your neighbor's wife and so make yourself unclean with her.

18:21 You shall not give any of your children to offer them to Molekh, and so profane the name of your Creator. I am YHWH.

18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman — it is an abomination.

18:24 Do not make yourselves unclean by any of these things, for by all these the nations I am driving out before you have become unclean,

18:25 And the land became unclean, so that I punished its iniquity, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.

18:26 But you shall keep my statutes and my rules and do none of these abominations, either the native or the stranger who sojourns among you —

18:28 Lest the land vomit you out when you make it unclean, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.

18:30 So keep my charge never to practice any of these abominable customs that were practiced before you, and never to make yourselves unclean by them. I am YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 18.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18:25 — the land became unclean, and the land vomited out its inhabitants. The land of Kena'an is not morally neutral territory that YHWH arbitrarily reassigns. The ancient record states that the prior inhabitants were vomited out by the land itself because of the practices of Chapter 18. The land has a covenant sensitivity to the behavior of those dwelling in it. This same principle applies to Yisra'EL — verse 18:28 warns that the land will vomit them out too if they practice these same things. The covenant connection between moral conduct and territorial possession runs throughout the Torah and the Nevi'im.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 19 The Holiness Code — Love Your Neighbor — The Practical Commandments

19:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

19:2 Speak to all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy.

19:3 Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father, and you shall keep my Sabbaths — I am YHWH your Creator.

19:4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves any gods of cast metal — I am YHWH your Creator.

19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap your field right up to its edge, neither shall you gather the gleanings after your harvest.

19:10 And you shall not strip your vineyard bare, neither shall you gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard. You shall leave them for the poor and for the sojourner — I am YHWH your Creator.

19:11 You shall not steal — you shall not deal falsely — you shall not lie to one another.

19:12 You shall not swear by my name falsely, and so profane the name of your Creator — I am YHWH.

19:13 You shall not oppress your neighbor or rob him. The wages of a hired worker shall not remain with you all night until the morning.

19:14 You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind, but you shall fear your Creator — I am YHWH.

19:15 You shall do no injustice in court. You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great, but in righteousness shall you judge your neighbor.

19:16 You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not stand up against the life of your neighbor — I am YHWH.

19:17 You shall not hate your brother in your heart, but you shall reason frankly with your neighbor, lest you incur sin because of him.

19:18 You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH.

19:26 You shall not eat any flesh with the blood in it. You shall not interpret omens or tell fortunes.

19:28 You shall not make any cuts on your body for the dead or tattoo yourselves — I am YHWH.

19:30 You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary — I am YHWH.

19:31 Do not turn to mediums or necromancers — do not seek them out, and so make yourselves unclean by them — I am YHWH your Creator.

19:32 You shall stand up before the gray head and honor the face of an old man, and you shall fear your Creator — I am YHWH.

19:33 When a stranger sojourns with you in your land, you shall not do him wrong.

19:34 You shall treat the stranger who sojourns with you as the native among you, and you shall love him as yourself, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt — I am YHWH your Creator.

19:35 You shall do no wrong in judgment, in measures of length or weight or quantity.

19:36 You shall have just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin — I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva, 4QLevb, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 19. The love your neighbor commandment — verse 19:18 — and the love the stranger commandment — verse 19:34 — are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:18 — you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH. This verse is the ethical center of the Holiness Code and is grounded in the character of YHWH — I am YHWH follows it directly. Love of neighbor is not a social contract principle derived from human philosophy. It is a covenant obligation whose authority is YHWH's own name and character. Verse 19:34 extends the same obligation to the stranger — love him as yourself, for you were strangers in Egypt. The Exodus experience is the ethical ground for treatment of the foreigner. Covenant memory generates covenant ethics.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:9-10 — the corner of the field and the fallen grapes left for the poor and the sojourner. The economic ethics of Vayikra are structural, not charitable. The harvest law does not ask the landowner to donate voluntarily. It prohibits harvesting the field's edge and the fallen gleanings. The poor have a built-in legal claim on a portion of every harvest. Covenant economics reserves a portion of production for those without land. This is not generosity — it is law.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 20 Penalties for Violations of the Holiness Code

20:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

20:2 Say to the people of Yisra'EL: Any one of the people of Yisra'EL or of the strangers who sojourn in Yisra'EL who gives any of his children to Molekh shall surely be put to death. The people of the land shall stone him with stones.

20:6 If a person turns to mediums and necromancers, whoring after them, I will set my face against that person and will cut him off from among his people.

20:7 Consecrate yourselves, therefore, and be holy, for I am YHWH your Creator.

20:8 Keep my statutes and do them — I am YHWH who sanctifies you.

20:22 You shall therefore keep all my statutes and all my rules and do them, that the land where I am bringing you to live may not vomit you out.

20:23 And you shall not walk in the customs of the nation that I am driving out before you, for they did all these things, and therefore I detested them.

20:24 But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land, and I will give it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I am YHWH your Creator, who has separated you from the peoples.

20:26 You shall be holy to me, for I YHWH am holy and have separated you from the peoples, that you should be mine.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 20.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20:26 — I have separated you from the peoples, that you should be mine. The separation of Yisra'EL from the nations is YHWH's act, not primarily Yisra'EL's cultural preference. YHWH separated them. The purpose is stated plainly: that you should be mine. This is covenant ownership language — the same language used in Shemot 19:5. The separation is not ethnic superiority. It is covenant assignment: a people set apart to belong to YHWH and bear his name and law among the nations.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 21 Laws for the Priests — Holiness Requirements for Aharon's Sons

21:1 And YHWH said to Moshe: Speak to the priests, the sons of Aharon, and say to them: No one shall make himself unclean for the dead among his people,

21:6 They shall be holy to their Creator and not profane the name of their Creator. For they offer YHWH's food offerings, the bread of their Creator — therefore they shall be holy.

21:7 They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled, neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband, for the priest is holy to his Creator.

21:8 You shall sanctify him, for he offers the bread of your Creator. He shall be holy to you, for I YHWH, who sanctify you, am holy.

21:10 The priest who is chief among his brothers, on whose head the anointing oil is poured and who has been consecrated to wear the garments, shall not let the hair of his head hang loose nor tear his clothes.

21:17 Speak to Aharon, saying: None of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the bread of his Creator.

21:23 But he shall not go through the veil or approach the altar, because he has a blemish, that he may not profane my sanctuaries, for I am YHWH who sanctifies them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 21.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The elevated purity requirements for the priesthood follow the same principle established in Chapter 10 at the death of Nadav and Avihu: nearness to YHWH's presence intensifies covenant obligation. The priests who offer at the altar and the high priest who enters the Most Holy Place on Yom Kippur are held to standards that exceed those applied to the general covenant population. The holiness of the sanctuary demands corresponding holiness in those who serve it.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 22 Holy Offerings — Who May Eat — The Unblemished Requirement

22:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

22:2 Tell Aharon and his sons to keep away from the holy things of the people of Yisra'EL, which they dedicate to me, so that they do not profane my holy name — I am YHWH.

22:17 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

22:18 Speak to Aharon and his sons and all the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When any one of the house of Yisra'EL or of the sojourners in Yisra'EL presents a burnt offering as his offering, for any of their vows or freewill offerings that they offer to YHWH,

22:19 If it is to be accepted for you it shall be a male without blemish, of the bulls or the sheep or the goats.

22:20 You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable for you.

22:29 And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to YHWH, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.

22:31 So you shall keep my commandments and do them — I am YHWH.

22:32 And you shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Yisra'EL. I am YHWH who sanctifies you,

22:33 Who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your Creator — I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 22.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22:32-33 — I am YHWH who sanctifies you, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your Creator. The Holiness Code consistently closes its legislative sections with this dual self-identification of YHWH: the one who sanctifies, and the one who brought the people out of Egypt. Holiness is not self-generated. It is imputed by YHWH through the covenant. And the ground of every covenant obligation traces back to the Exodus — the concrete historical event that established YHWH as Creator of Yisra'EL by act, not merely by declaration.

THE FEASTS OF YHWH — CHAPTERS 23 THROUGH 25

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 23 The Appointed Times of YHWH — The Mo'adim

23:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

23:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: These are the appointed feasts of YHWH that you shall proclaim as holy convocations — they are my appointed feasts.

23:3 Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, a holy convocation. You shall do no work. It is a Sabbath to YHWH in all your dwelling places.

23:4 These are the appointed feasts of YHWH, the holy convocations, which you shall proclaim at the time appointed for them.

23:5 In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is YHWH's Pesach.

23:6 And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to YHWH — for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.

23:9 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

23:10 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When you come into the land that I give you and reap its harvest, you shall bring the sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest to the priest,

23:11 And he shall wave the sheaf before YHWH, so that you may be accepted. On the day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.

23:15 You shall count seven full weeks from the day after the Sabbath, from the day that you brought the sheaf of the wave offering.

23:16 You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath. Then you shall present a grain offering of new grain to YHWH.

23:21 And you shall make a proclamation on the same day. You shall hold a holy convocation. You shall not do any ordinary work. It is a statute forever in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.

23:23 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

23:24 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a day of solemn rest, a memorial proclaimed with blast of trumpets, a holy convocation.

23:26 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

23:27 Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. It shall be for you a time of holy convocation, and you shall afflict yourselves and present a food offering to YHWH.

23:28 And you shall not do any work on that very day, for it is a Day of Atonement, to make atonement for you before YHWH your Creator.

23:34 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month and for seven days is the Feast of Booths to YHWH.

23:40 And you shall take on the first day the fruit of splendid trees, branches of palm trees and boughs of leafy trees and willows of the brook, and you shall rejoice before YHWH your Creator seven days.

23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days. All native Yisra'ELis shall dwell in booths,

23:43 That your generations may know that I made the people of Yisra'EL dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt — I am YHWH your Creator.

23:44 Thus Moshe declared to the people of Yisra'EL the appointed feasts of YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLevb, 4QLevc, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 23. The feast calendar is attested in multiple Cave 4 manuscripts. The Book of Yovelim — physically found at Qumran — contains an extensive parallel feast calendar that in several points presents a different calendar reckoning than the Masoretic, following a 364-day solar calendar rather than a lunar-solar calendar. The DSS community's calendar observance followed Yovelim's solar reckoning.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. The Ethiopian Beta Israel community observes all seven of the appointed feasts listed in this chapter — Sabbath, Pesach, Unleavened Bread, Shavuot, Yom Teru'ah, Yom Kippur, and Sukkot — as central covenant obligations, consistent with the ancient Vayikra legislation and independent of Rabbinic calendar development.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text and the DSS agree closely on the feast legislation of Chapter 23. The calendar dispute — solar versus lunar-solar — is a DSS community position preserved in Yovelim and the Temple Scroll rather than a variant in the Vayikra text itself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23:2 — these are my appointed feasts. The word translated appointed feasts is mo'adim — appointed times. The same word used for the tent of meeting — ohel mo'ed — the tent of appointed meeting. The feasts are not human religious commemorations added to the covenant. They are YHWH's appointments — times he designates for the covenant people to meet with him in specific ways through specific acts. They are his feasts — he says so twice in verse 23:2. The covenant calendar belongs to YHWH, not to the nation's religious tradition. The people observe what YHWH has appointed.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23:43 — that your generations may know that I made the people of Yisra'EL dwell in booths when I brought them out of Egypt. Sukkot — the Feast of Booths — is an enacted memory. For seven days the covenant people live in temporary shelters and re-inhabit the wilderness experience of dependence on YHWH. The feast is a curriculum for every generation. Children ask why they are living in booths. The answer is the Exodus. The feasts are the primary intergenerational education system of the covenant people.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 24 The Lampstand Oil — The Showbread — The Blasphemer

24:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

24:2 Command the people of Yisra'EL to bring you pure oil from beaten olives for the lamp, that a light may be kept burning regularly.

24:3 Outside the veil of the testimony, in the tent of meeting, Aharon shall arrange it from evening to morning before YHWH regularly. It shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.

24:5 You shall take fine flour and bake twelve loaves from it — two tenths of an ephah shall be in each loaf.

24:6 And you shall set them in two rows, six in a row, on the table of pure gold before YHWH.

24:7 And you shall put pure frankincense on each row, that it may go with the bread as a memorial portion as a food offering to YHWH.

24:8 Every Sabbath day Aharon shall arrange it before YHWH regularly — it is from the people of Yisra'EL as a covenant forever.

24:10 Now an Israelite woman's son, whose father was an Egyptian, went out among the people of Yisra'EL. And the Israelite woman's son and a man of Yisra'EL fought in the camp.

24:11 And the Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name and cursed. Then they brought him to Moshe. His mother's name was Shelomit, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.

24:14 Bring out of the camp the one who cursed, and let all who heard him lay their hands on his head, and let all the congregation stone him.

24:15 And speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: Whoever curses his Creator shall bear his sin.

24:16 Whoever blasphemes the name of YHWH shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall stone him. The sojourner as well as the native, when he blasphemes the Name, shall be put to death.

24:17 Whoever takes a human life shall surely be put to death.

24:19 If anyone injures his neighbor, as he has done it shall be done to him.

24:20 Fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth — whatever injury he has given a person shall be given to him.

24:22 You shall have the same rule for the sojourner and for the native, for I am YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLevb and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 24.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24:22 — you shall have the same rule for the sojourner and for the native. The covenant law applies equally to the stranger and the native Yisra'ELi. The same justice standard, the same covenant protection, the same legal accountability. This principle of equal application of covenant law to both native and sojourner runs throughout the Torah and is stated repeatedly in Shemot, Vayikra, Bamidbar, and Devarim. The covenant community is not a closed ethnic legal system. Those who sojourn within it are subject to the same law and protected by the same law.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 25 The Shemitah — The Jubilee — The Redemption of Land and Persons

25:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe on Mount Sinai, saying:

25:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When you come into the land that I give you, the land shall keep a Sabbath to YHWH.

25:3 For six years you shall sow your field, and for six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather in its fruits,

25:4 But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to YHWH. You shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.

25:8 You shall count seven weeks of years, seven times seven years, so that the time of the seven weeks of years shall give you forty-nine years.

25:9 Then you shall sound the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month. On the Day of Atonement you shall sound the trumpet throughout all your land.

25:10 And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, when each of you shall return to his property and each of you shall return to his clan.

25:13 In this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his property.

25:14 And if you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor, you shall not wrong one another.

25:17 You shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your Creator, for I am YHWH your Creator.

25:23 The land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine. For you are strangers and sojourners with me.

25:24 And in all the country you possess, you shall allow a redemption of the land.

25:25 If your brother becomes poor and sells part of his property, then his nearest redeemer shall come and redeem what his brother has sold.

25:35 If your brother becomes poor and cannot maintain himself with you, you shall support him as though he were a stranger and a sojourner, and he shall live with you.

25:36 Take no interest from him or profit, but fear your Creator, that your brother may live beside you.

25:37 You shall not lend him your money at interest, nor give him your food for profit.

25:38 I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Kena'an, and to be your Creator.

25:39 If your brother becomes poor beside you and sells himself to you, you shall not make him serve as a slave.

25:40 He shall be with you as a hired worker and as a sojourner. He shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.

25:42 For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt — they shall not be sold as slaves.

25:43 You shall not rule over him ruthlessly but shall fear your Creator.

25:55 For it is to me that the people of Yisra'EL are servants. They are my servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt — I am YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLevb, 4QLevc, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 25. The jubilee legislation is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The Book of Yovelim — the name itself derived from the word for jubilee — is the extensive DSS expansion of the jubilee calendar and its theological significance, physically confirmed at Qumran in fifteen manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full. The Orit and the Book of Yovelim are the primary authorities for the jubilee legislation in the Ethiopian Beta Israel canon.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:23 — the land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine. YHWH's declaration of land ownership is the theological foundation of the entire jubilee system. No Yisra'ELi actually owns the land — they hold it in trust from YHWH. Every fifty years the trust reverts. Accumulated land wealth cannot become permanent dynastic holdings across generations. The jubilee is the structural economic reset built into the covenant law — every fiftieth year, land returns to the original covenant allotment of each clan, debt is released, and those who sold themselves into bonded service go free. The jubilee is the most radical economic legislation in the ancient world. It is not charity — it is covenant law.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:42 — for they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. They shall not be sold as slaves. The Exodus is the ground of the prohibition on permanent slavery of fellow Yisra'ELis. YHWH already owns them — they belong to him through the Exodus. No human being can own as a permanent slave someone who already belongs to YHWH. The redemption from Egypt creates a covenant status that no subsequent transaction can permanently override.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 26 The Covenant Blessings and Curses — The Terms of the Covenant

26:1 You shall not make idols for yourselves or erect an image or pillar, and you shall not set up a figured stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am YHWH your Creator.

26:2 You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary — I am YHWH.

26:3 If you walk in my statutes and observe my commandments and do them,

26:4 Then I will give you your rains in their season, and the land shall yield its increase, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.

26:6 I will give peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and none shall make you afraid. And I will remove harmful beasts from the land, and the sword shall not go through your land.

26:9 And I will turn to you and make you fruitful and multiply you and will confirm my covenant with you.

26:11 I will make my dwelling among you, and my soul shall not abhor you.

26:12 And I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people.

26:13 I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that you should not be their slaves. And I have broken the bars of your yoke and made you walk erect.

26:14 But if you will not listen to me and will not do all these commandments,

26:15 If you spurn my statutes, and if your soul abhors my rules, so that you will not do all my commandments, but break my covenant,

26:16 Then I will do this to you: I will visit you with panic, with wasting disease and fever that consume the eyes and make the heart ache. And you shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.

26:18 And if in spite of this you will not listen to me, then I will discipline you again sevenfold for your sins.

26:27 But if in spite of this you will not listen to me, but walk contrary to me,

26:33 And I will scatter you among the nations, and I will unsheathe the sword after you, and your land shall be a desolation, and your cities shall be a waste.

26:40 But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers in their treachery that they committed against me, and also in walking contrary to me,

26:41 So that I walked contrary to them and brought them into the land of their enemies — if then their uncircumcised heart is humbled and they make amends for their iniquity,

26:42 Then I will remember my covenant with Yaakov, and I will remember my covenant with Yitzhak and my covenant with Avraham, and I will remember the land.

26:44 Yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not spurn them, neither will I abhor them so as to destroy them utterly and break my covenant with them, for I am YHWH their Creator.

26:45 But I will for their sake remember the covenant with their forefathers, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations, that I might be their Creator — I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLevb, 4QLevc, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 26. The covenant blessings and curses are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The structure of blessing and curse tied to covenant observance is parallel to the formal structure of ancient Near Eastern suzerainty treaties, confirming the covenant-treaty nature of the Torah's legal framework.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26:42-45 — I will remember my covenant with Yaakov, with Yitzhak, with Avraham. Even in the severest covenant discipline — exile among the enemies — YHWH does not cancel the Avrahamic covenant. The covenant with the patriarchs is not conditional on the behavior of their descendants. It is YHWH's sworn commitment to himself. The curses of Chapter 26 can be activated by covenant breach. The covenant itself cannot be annulled. This is the theological architecture that sustains the prophetic hope throughout the Nevi'im: no matter how severe the exile, YHWH will remember the covenant and restore. The curses of Vayikra 26 and Devarim 28 describe what happens. The covenant of Bereshit 15 describes what ultimately prevails.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 27 Vows and Dedications — The Conclusion of Vayikra

27:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

27:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: If anyone makes a special vow to YHWH involving the valuation of persons,

27:3 Then the valuation of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.

27:9 If the vow involves an animal that may be offered as an offering to YHWH, all of it that is given to YHWH is holy.

27:14 When a man dedicates his house as a holy gift to YHWH, the priest shall value it as either good or bad. As the priest values it, so it shall stand.

27:16 If a man dedicates to YHWH part of the land that is his possession, then the valuation shall be in proportion to its seed. A homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.

27:26 But a firstborn of animals, which as a firstborn belongs to YHWH, no man may dedicate — whether ox or sheep, it is YHWH's.

27:28 But no devoted thing that a man devotes to YHWH, of anything that he has, whether man or beast, or of his inherited field, shall be sold or redeemed — every devoted thing is most holy to YHWH.

27:30 Every tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the trees, is YHWH's — it is holy to YHWH.

27:32 And every tithe of herds and flocks, every tenth animal of all that pass under the herdsman's staff, shall be holy to YHWH.

27:34 These are the commandments that YHWH commanded Moshe for the people of Yisra'EL on Mount Sinai.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLevb and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 27. The closing verse — these are the commandments that YHWH commanded Moshe for the people of Yisra'EL on Mount Sinai — is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 27:34 — these are the commandments that YHWH commanded Moshe for the people of Yisra'EL on Mount Sinai. Vayikra closes with the same closing formula as Shemot — tracing all legislation back to Mount Sinai and the direct command of YHWH. The entire legal corpus of Vayikra — offerings, priesthood, purity, holiness code, feasts, jubilee, blessings and curses, vows — is declared to have one source and one location: YHWH at Sinai through Moshe. The third book of the Torah begins with YHWH calling from the tent of meeting and ends with YHWH commanding from Mount Sinai. The legislation bridges the two locations of YHWH's self-disclosure in the wilderness: the mountain where the covenant was given and the tent where YHWH now dwells among the people.

END OF VAYIKRA — VOLUME THREE COMPLETE

The Third Book of the Torah — Chapters 1 through 27
